chart.title: Child mortality rate
chart.subtitle: Share of newborns who die before reaching the age of five.

Child mortality rate - Sex: all - Varian.descriptionShort: The share of children who die before reaching the age of 5.
descriptionKey: This is the *probability* a newborn would die before reaching the age of five if the year's age-specific mortality rates stayed unchanged. It is not the share of an actual cohort that has been observed dying.
Child mortality rate - Sex: all - Varian.descriptionProcessing: The UN publishes infant and under-five mortality as deaths per 1,000 live births. We divide by 10 to express the values as a percentage of newborns.
Child mortality rate - Sex: all - Varian.citationShort: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data
Child mortality rate - Sex: all - Varian.citationLong: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data. “Child mortality rate – UN WPP” [dataset]. United Nations, “World Population Prospects”; United Nations, “World Population Prospects - Interim Update” [original data].
